tests_added.name: apply_throughput_headers_adds_autoscale_throughput
tests_added.description: Verifies that autoscale ThroughputProperties produces the OFFER_AUTOPILOT_SETTINGS header (not OFFER_THROUGHPUT) and that the header value contains the configured max throughput. The existing 5 tests only covered manual throughput, leaving the autoscale code path untested.
tests_added.cross_sdk_source: Java CosmosDatabaseTest.createDatabase_withPropertiesThroughputAndOptions and .NET CosmosDatabaseTests both test creating databases with throughput options. Both SDKs support manual and autoscale throughput, validating that the correct header is emitted.

tests_already_adequate.name: apply_throughput_headers_none_returns_unchanged
tests_already_adequate.covers: R6: no throughput → options pass through unchanged

tests_already_adequate.name: apply_throughput_headers_empty_offer_returns_unchanged
tests_already_adequate.covers: R6: default ThroughputProperties → no headers emitted

tests_already_adequate.name: apply_throughput_headers_adds_manual_throughput
tests_already_adequate.covers: R6: manual throughput → OFFER_THROUGHPUT header added

tests_already_adequate.name: apply_throughput_headers_preserves_existing_custom_headers
tests_already_adequate.covers: R6/AC3: throughput headers merge with existing custom headers

tests_already_adequate.name: apply_throughput_headers_user_headers_take_precedence
tests_already_adequate.covers: R6/AC3: user custom headers are not overwritten by throughput headers

tests_excluded.description: Trivial default-value assertions on options types (e.g., CreateDatabaseOptions::default().throughput.is_none())
tests_excluded.reason: Anti-pattern: tests obvious behavior of derived Default trait with no logic
Anti-pattern: trivial setter tests that mirror implementation. The setter just assigns a field.

tests_excluded.description: Round-trip serialization of RequestBody { id } used in create_database
tests_excluded.reason: Anti-pattern: explicitly excluded in instructions. serde_json::to_vec on a simple struct is not worth testing.
Requires emulator. The existing emulator CRUD tests already validate create/delete lifecycle. Conflict testing is a service-behavior test, not a cutover validation.

tests_excluded.description: Request-charge assertions on responses (Java createDatabase_withProperties pattern)
tests_excluded.reason: Requires emulator. Request charge parsing is already tested in driver_response_headers_to_headers tests. The cutover doesn't change charge handling.

integration_test_note: Existing emulator tests (cosmos_databases.rs, cosmos_containers.rs) are the primary validation that all 4 methods correctly route through the driver pipeline. They cover create/read/delete database, create container with throughput, and container with hierarchical partition keys.

patterns_found: CreateDatabaseAsync with throughput → ReadThroughputAsync → verify value
CreateDatabaseAsync without throughput → ReadThroughputAsync → expect NotFound
CreateContainerAsync under created database → verify operations chain
